revision.text:
[[ja:荒木田氏]] {{Japanese clans |Name = Arakida clan |Crest_Name = |Ancestral_Name = Arakida Kannushi |Founder = {{ill|Amanomito no Mikoto|ja|天見通命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomito no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}}
{{ill|Kazoku|en|Kazoku|qid=Q241734|lt=Kazoku|1=Kazoku}} ({{ill|Baron|en|Baron|qid=Q165503|lt=Baron|1=Baron}}) |Place_Of_Origin = Unknown |Base = {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|qid=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}}[[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]]
{{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}{{ill|Uji-Yamada City|ja|宇治山田市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Yamada City|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢市|1=Ise, Mie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q328067}}{{ill|Miyago Town|ja|宮後町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyago Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Notable_People = {{ill|Fujinami Ujitsune|ja|藤波氏経|lt=Arakida (Fujinami) Ujitsune|lt_ja=荒木田（藤波）氏経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujinami Ujitsune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Arakida Moritake|en|Arakida Moritake|qid=Q625505|lt=Arakida Moritake|1=Arakida Moritake}}
{{ill|Sonoda Moriyoshi|ja|薗田守良|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonoda Moriyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Branches = {{ill|Ichimon|ja|一門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichimon|1=ichimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11353240}}
{{ill|Sawada family|ja|沢田家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawada family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒木田氏|1=Arakida clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11618716}} ({{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|qid=Q11588367|lt=Shrine family|1=Shake (social class)}})
{{ill|Sonoda family (Arakida clan)|ja|薗田家 (荒木田氏)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonoda family (Arakida clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shinto priest family)
{{ill|Imeno family|ja|井面家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imeno family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shinto priest family)
{{ill|Nimon|ja|二門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nimon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Imuko family|ja|井向家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imuko family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shinto priest family)
{{ill|Nakagawa family (Arakida clan)|ja|中川家 (荒木田氏)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakagawa family (Arakida clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shinto priest family)
{{ill|Fujinami family (Arakida clan)|ja|藤波家 (荒木田氏)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujinami family (Arakida clan)|1=Fujinami family (Arakida clan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q121237527}} (Shinto priest family)
Other
{{ill|Tsutsumi family (Arakida clan)|ja|堤家 (荒木田氏)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsutsumi family (Arakida clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shinto priest family)
{{ill|Seki family|ja|世木家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shinto priest family)
{{ill|Sabachi family|ja|佐八家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabachi family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shinto priest family)
Many others }} '''Arakida clan''' (荒木田氏) was a {{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|qid=Q11588367|lt=Shrine family|1=Shake (social class)}} and {{ill|Kazoku|en|Kazoku|qid=Q241734|lt=Kazoku|1=Kazoku}} family in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The clan traditionally served as hereditary {{ill|Kannushi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannushi|qq=}} for the {{ill|Naikū of Ise Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮宮内宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikū of Ise Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the main branch, the Sawada family, was raised to the Rank of baron (男爵) within the {{ill|Kazoku system|ja|華族制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazoku system|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 華族|1=Kazoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q241734}} and later restored the surname Arakida during the Taisho era {{sfn|Morioka Hiroshi|2012|p=33|qq=}}. __TOC__ {{clear|qq=}} == Overview == The Arakida clan is said to have descended from the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|qid=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan|1=Nakatomi clan}} and received the Arakida surname during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|qid=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}}. The clan later split into two main branches, Ichimon and Nimon, which were further divided into several families (e.g., Sawada, Sonoda, Imeno, Nakagawa, and Fujinami families). Some families held the right to serve as full Kannushi (重代家, Jingū family), while others were restricted to sub-Kannushi (地下権任家) positions {{sfn|Morioka Hiroshi|2012|p=33|qq=}}. The Sawada family was the main branch {{sfn|Morioka Hiroshi|2012|p=33|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, Sawada Yasutsuna, the head of the Sawada family, submitted four petitions for inclusion in the Kazoku system, all of which were initially rejected {{sfn|Matsuda Takeyuki|2015|p=339|qq=}}. His adopted heir, Sawada Koichiro, renewed the petition in 1889 (Meiji 22). According to the Ministry of the Imperial Household's examination document dated July 1, 1890, the Sawada family was deemed superior to other branches of the Arakida clan in lineage and status, being classified as a "Jingū family" with a history of service as full Kannushi. Financially, the family was shown to possess substantial wealth, including land valued at over 500 yen and government bonds exceeding 6,000 yen, as evidenced by their income tax records. Based on these findings, their ennoblement was approved by {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|qid=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji|1=Emperor Meiji}} on July 17, and the title of baron (男爵) was officially granted on August 27 {{sfn|Matsuda Takeyuki|2015|p=339|qq=}}. Koichiro later changed his name to Arakida Taikoku in 1891 (Meiji 24) and restored the Arakida surname during the Taisho era {{sfn|Morioka Hiroshi|2012|p=33|qq=}}{{sfn|Kazoku Daikan|1990|p=454|qq=}}. During the early Showa period, the Arakida baronial family's residence was located in {{ill|Miyago Town, Uji-Yamada City|ja|三重県宇治山田市宮後町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyago Town, Uji-Yamada City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{sfn|Kazoku Daikan|1990|p=453|qq=}}. == Notes == {{Notes|qq=}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|date=1990th year （平成2nd year ）|title=Kazoku Daikan|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Publishing Association|publisher={{ill|Japan Book Center|ja|日本図書センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Book Center|1=Nihon Tosho Sentā|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11507561}}|isbn=978-4820540342|ref=harv|qq=}} * {{Cite book|和書|date=2015th year （平成27th year ）|title=Dictionary of Petitioners for Kazoku Ranks|author=Matsuda Takeyuki|authorlink={{ill|Matsuda Takeyuki|ja|松田敬之|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuda Takeyuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4346755}}|isbn=978-4642014724|ref=harv|qq=}} * {{Cite book|和書|date=2012nd year （平成24th year ）|title=Dictionary of Distinguished Japanese Families|author=Morioka Hiroshi|authorlink={{ill|Morioka Hiroshi|ja|森岡浩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morioka Hiroshi|1=Hiroshi Morioka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q23946788}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11524645}}|isbn=978-4490108217|ref=harv|qq=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|qid=Q11588367|lt=Shrine family|1=Shake (social class)}} * {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Kosha Shrine|ja|小社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosha Shrine|1=Ogosō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17224784}} {{Redirect categories |redirect1=Arakida family |1-1=Baronial families of Japan |redirect2=Sawada family |2-1=Baronial families of Japan }} == References == {{translated page|ja|荒木田氏|version=103422875|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:荒木田氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11618716}} [[Category:Ise Shrine]] [[Category:Japanese clans]]
